enonce.texte:
Read the announcement below and answer the questions that follow.

VOLUNTEERS NEEDED!

Peace Club Cameroon is looking for young volunteers to help with conflict resolution in our community. We need students who are passionate about peace and justice.

Activities:

  • Organize peace workshops in schools
  • Mediate conflicts between students
  • Write articles about peace for the school newspaper
  • Participate in community dialogue sessions

Requirements: Must be between 14-18 years old, have good communication skills, and be available on weekends.

Contact: Send your application to peaceclub.cameroon@gmail.com or call 6 77 88 99 00.
images: A group of diverse young Cameroonian students in school uniforms, some holding a banner that says 'Peace Club', standing together and smiling in front of a school building.

questions.texte: What is the name of the organization that is looking for volunteers?
List three activities that volunteers will do.
questions.reponse:
1. Organize peace workshops in schools
2. Mediate conflicts between students
3. Write articles about peace for the school newspaper

questions.texte: What are the requirements to become a volunteer?
questions.reponse: Must be between 14-18 years old, have good communication skills, and be available on weekends.

questions.texte: Why do you think organizations like Peace Club Cameroon are important?
questions.reponse: Organizations like Peace Club Cameroon are important because they help create a peaceful environment in schools and communities. They teach young people how to resolve conflicts peacefully, which reduces violence and promotes mutual understanding.

intitule: Filling Out a Volunteer Application Form

enonce.texte:
Complete the following volunteer application form with the correct information about yourself.

VOLUNTEER APPLICATION FORM

PEACE CLUB CAMEROON

Personal Information:

Full Name: _________________________

Date of Birth: _________________________

School: _________________________

Class: _________________________

Phone Number: _________________________

Email: _________________________

Why do you want to volunteer?

_________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________

What skills do you have that can help?

_________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________
images: A simple illustration of a clipboard with a pen, representing a form being filled out, placed next to a stack of papers.

questions.texte: Complete the volunteer application form with your personal information.
questions.reponse: This is a personal response task. Students should fill in their name, date of birth, school, class, phone number, and email.

sousQuestions.texte: Write a paragraph explaining why you want to volunteer and what skills you can bring.
sousQuestions.reponse: This is a creative writing task. Student's answer should be a paragraph of 50-80 words. Example: 'I want to volunteer because I believe in peace and want to help my school community. I am a good listener and I can communicate well with others. I am also good at organizing activities and working in a team.'

intitule: Writing a Friendly Letter to a Pen Pal

enonce.texte:
Imagine you are writing a letter to your pen pal in another country. Tell them about your experience volunteering for conflict resolution in your school. Use the format of a friendly letter.

Format of a Friendly Letter:

  • Heading: Your address and date
  • Salutation: Dear [Name],
  • Body: Write about your volunteer experience
  • Closing: Your friend, [Your name]
images: A blank piece of paper with a pen next to it, with small decorative elements like hearts and stars around it, representing a friendly letter.

questions.texte: Write a friendly letter to your pen pal about your volunteer experience in conflict resolution.
questions.reponse:
This is a creative writing task. Example response:
P.O. Box 456
Yaoundé, Cameroon
June 17, 2026

Dear Kofi,

I hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to tell you about something exciting I have been doing. I joined the Peace Club at my school, and we are volunteering to help resolve conflicts between students. Last week, I helped two friends who were arguing. I listened to both of them and helped them understand each other. It felt great to bring peace. I think this is important work because when there is peace, everyone can learn better. I hope you are also doing something meaningful in your community.

Write back soon!

Your friend,
Mary

intitule: Dialogue: Asking and Answering About Volunteering

enonce.texte:
Complete the dialogue between two students, Amina and David, about volunteering for conflict resolution. Then, create your own dialogue with a partner.

Complete the Dialogue

Amina: Hi David! Have you heard about the Peace Club volunteer program?
David: Yes, I have. I'm thinking of joining. _____________ (Are you / Will you / Do you) interested too?
Amina: Yes, I am. I want to help resolve conflicts at school. _____________ (What / Why / How) do you want to volunteer?
David: I think it's important to help others. Also, I want to learn new skills. _____________ (When / Where / Who) do we sign up?
Amina: We can sign up at the school office. The coordinator is Mr. Mbappe. _____________ (Can / Must / Will) we work together?
David: Yes, that would be great! Let's go and sign up now.
Amina: OK, let's go!
images: Two teenagers, a boy and a girl, sitting on a bench at school, having a conversation. They are holding backpacks and appear engaged in a friendly discussion.

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the correct question words or auxiliaries.

sousQuestions.texte: Create a similar dialogue with a partner. One of you asks about volunteering, and the other answers. Include at least 5 exchanges.
sousQuestions.reponse:
Example of expected dialogue:
Student A: Hi! What are you doing this weekend?
Student B: I'm going to volunteer for the Peace Club.
Student A: Really? What will you do?
Student B: We will organize a peace workshop for younger students.
Student A: That sounds interesting! Can I join?
Student B: Yes, of course! Come with me on Saturday.

enonce.texte: Write questions and answers about volunteering for conflict resolution. Use the prompts provided to create complete sentences.

questions.texte: Write a question using the words: 'you / want / volunteer / conflict resolution / ?'
questions.reponse: Do you want to volunteer for conflict resolution?

questions.texte: Write a question using: 'you / help / resolve / conflicts / school / ?'
questions.reponse: Do you help resolve conflicts at school?
Yes, I do. / Yes, I help resolve conflicts at school.

questions.texte: Write a question using: 'when / you / start / volunteering / ?'
Answer the question in exercise 5: 'I _____________ last month.'

enonce.texte: Match the words on the left with their definitions on the right. Write the correct letter next to each number.
images: A simple matching exercise illustration with two columns: one with words and one with definitions, connected by lines.

questions.texte: Match the words with their definitions:

sousQuestions.reponse: d. A person who works without being paid
a. A serious disagreement or argument
e. To help two sides reach an agreement
b. The solution to a problem
c. A group of people living in the same area
